summary: Completed full backend + frontend testing of 9x.design studio website. Backend: 9/9 pytest tests passed covering /api/health, /api/contact (happy path, validation, service normalization, persistence), /api/leads (no _id leakage). Frontend: 10/11 manual UI checks passed covering navbar, pricing, FAQ accordion, portfolio modal, WhatsApp button, hero, testimonials, sections, contact form submission (end-to-end), client-side email validation. One minor UX gap noted (textarea min-length not enforced client-side — backend will 422).

minor.issue: Admin/debug endpoint with no auth — fine for now as noted in review_request, but should be protected before production.

integration_issues.issue: Textarea has no minLength=5 attribute; frontend only relies on `required`. Typing 4 chars passes client-side but triggers backend 422. Recommend adding minLength={5} on textarea and/or a user-visible error from axios error handler (currently user sees generic failure).

action_items: (Optional) Add minLength={5} to textarea in Contact.tsx and show inline error when backend returns 422.
(Optional) Protect /api/leads endpoint with auth before shipping to production.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: Legacy /api/status endpoint still present — remove if unused.
server.py: /api/leads returns all leads without auth/pagination safety; consider auth + max-limit cap.
Contact.tsx: textarea should have minLength attribute to match backend validation (min 5 chars).

Email f-string HTML in server.py is fine; note special chars in user name could break layout (not currently escaped) — consider html.escape() on lead.name/message before injecting into HTML template (XSS is not user-facing but email-clients can vary).
RESEND_API_KEY empty: email_sent = False flow is working as designed; user aware.
seed_data_creation: Created a few TEST_-prefixed leads in MongoDB `nine_x_design.leads` via the public /api/contact endpoint during testing (TEST_John Doe, TEST_Jane, TEST_Min, TEST_Playwright User).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Frontend is Vite. Contact form fields use data-testids: contact-name-input, contact-email-input, contact-service-select, contact-budget-select, contact-company-input, contact-message-input, contact-submit-btn (NOT name/id attrs). After a successful submit, the form replaces itself with a 'Message Sent!' success card — to reuse form, click 'Send another message →'. HTML5 email validation works; message length is only backend-validated. RESEND_API_KEY is intentionally empty so email_sent is always False — this is expected.
rca of the issue: N/A — no blocking issues. Only minor improvement: textarea minLength attribute missing; causes backend 422 if user types <5 chars of message.
